title: High-Altitude Cooking
content:
HIGH-ALTITUDE COOKING

ADJUSTMENTS REQUIRED

Water boils at lower temperatures as altitude increases (212°F sea level; 203°F at 5,000 ft; 194°F at 10,000 ft). Any process involving liquid lengthens proportionally.

BAKING

Decrease leavening slightly (baking powder/soda). Increase liquid and/or raise oven temperature 10-15°F. Basic cake recipes for high altitudes require modifications.

PRESSURE COOKING

Maintain same timing as sea level but increase pressure ½ pound for every 1,000-foot rise using specially calibrated gauge.

VEGETABLES

Use approximately same temperatures for baking. For moist-heat cooking: more liquid and longer time required. Slice thinly or cut small to reduce time.

Time adjustments: For each 1,000 feet elevation, add 10% for whole beets, carrots, onions; 7% for green beans, squash, cabbage, turnips, parsnips.

Frozen vegetables: Whole carrots and beans may need 5-12 minutes additional; others only 1-2 minutes.

ROASTING

Procedures do not differ materially from sea level.
